contraindications.clinicalRationale: Limited data for severe CKD. Oral semaglutide specifically not recommended with eGFR <15 ml/min per 1.73 m² due to insufficient safety and efficacy data in this population.
contraindications.riskDescription: Unknown safety profile in severe kidney dysfunction
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering agents with established safety in severe CKD
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Semaglutide (oral): 3 mg, 7 mg, or 14 mg daily; CKD adjustment: No dosage adjustment; Limited data for severe CKD, not recommended with eGFR <15 ml/min per 1.73 m²

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

conditionDescriptions: Type 2 diabetes with DPP-4 inhibitor use
contraindications.clinicalRationale: GLP-1 RA should not be used in combination with DPP-4 inhibitors due to overlapping mechanisms of action and potential for increased adverse effects without additional benefit.
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk of adverse effects without therapeutic benefit due to overlapping mechanisms
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Practice Point 4.2.3: GLP-1 RA should not be used in combination with dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitors.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Limited data available for severe chronic kidney disease patients
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with severe chronic kidney disease
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor kidney function and consider dose adjustment or alternative therapy in patients with severe CKD

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Risk of hypoglycemia increased when used concomitantly with sulfonylureas or insulin
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients receiving concurrent sulfonylurea or insulin therapy
warnings.actionRequired: Doses of sulfonylurea and/or insulin may need to be reduced when initiating semaglutide

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Gastrointestinal side effects may occur, particularly during initiation
warnings.actionRequired: Start with low dose and titrate up slowly to minimize gastrointestinal side effects

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
